introSection:
Chimelong Paradise is a major amusement park in Panyu District, Guangzhou, Guangdong, China. Opened on April 12, 2006, Chimelong Paradise is the largest amusement park in China and boasts the 10 Inversion Roller Coaster, which prior to the opening of The Smiler at Alton Towers in Staffordshire, England, shared the record for most inversions with Colossus at Thorpe Park in Surrey, England. It is classified as a AAAAA scenic area by the China National Tourism Administration.

Chimelong Paradise is part of Guangzhou Chimelong Tourist Resort which is operated by the Chimelong Group, a Chinese tourism company that also operates numerous other attractions, including Chimelong Ocean Kingdom, the Chimelong Safari Park and Chimelong International Circus. Chimelong Group reported that construction of the park cost over 1 billion RMB. Stretching over 60 hectares, the park currently contains more than 60 attractions and has a daily capacity of 50,000 visitors. In 2008, a new roller coaster opened that is nearly 61 m tall and reaches a maximum speed of 105 km/h. The park is linked by a bridge to Chimelong Water Park, and some admission tickets include access to both parks.

On July 29, 2014, rare giant panda cub triplets, all three of which have thus far survived, were born at the Chimelong Safari Park.

sections.title: Themed areas
sections.content: Chimelong Paradise is split into seven different themed areas with the following attractions.

sections.title: Whirlwind Island
sections.content:
* Half Pipe
 * Rotating Cup
 * Swiftly Chair
 * Sombrero
 * Bumper Car
 * Music Flier

sections.title: Screaming Zone
sections.content:
* 10 Inversion Roller Coaster
 * Windshear
 * Motorbike Coaster
 * Jumping Machine
 * Sky Rocket Roller Coaster

sections.title: Happy Kingdom
sections.content:
* Convoy
 * Flying Tiger
 * Helicopter Tower
 * Double-deck Carousel
 * Jumping Around
 * Junior Jet
 * Samba Balloon
 * Fire Truck
 * North Pole
 * Sky Bicycle
 * Skater
 * Crazy Bus
 * Sky Drop
 * Sun & Moon Wheel
 * Jumping Tower

sections.title: Kids' Land
sections.content:
* Ball Shooting
 * Bravery Fire Fighting
 * Rotating Honeybee
 * Flying Saucer
 * Turtle Paradise
 * Noah's Ark
 * Canoe River
 * Mini Tug
 * Kids Climbing Vehicle
 * Small Swing
 * Circus Train
 * 12 Horse Carousel

sections.title: Rainbow Bay
sections.content:
* Dive Coaster
 * Giant Frisbee
 * Young Star Coaster

sections.title: Water World
sections.content:
* Splash Battle
 * Bumper Boat
 * Shoot the Chute
 * Pirate Raft

sections.title: Phantom Zone
sections.content:
* Rio (4D movie)
 * Forest Temple
 * Rain Forest
 * Alien Attack

sections.title: Transportation
sections.content:
Guangzhou Metro:
 * Exit E of Hanxi Changlong Station on Line 3 and Line 7 are linked by a footbridge (3 minute walk) or free shuttle bus

Buses:
 * Metro Line 9 feeder bus Hanxi Changlong Station - Panyu Village
 * 129 Jincheng Garden (Dongfeng East)
 * 302 Guangzhou East Railway Station
 * 303 Tianhe Bus Station
 * 304 Tangxia
 * 310 University City
 * 657 Dongpu Terminal

sections.content:

Chimelong Paradise is a major amusement park in Panyu District, Guangzhou, Guangdong, China. Opened on April 12, 2006, Chimelong Paradise is the largest amusement park in China and boasts the 10 Inversion Roller Coaster, which prior to the opening of The Smiler at Alton Towers in Staffordshire, England, shared the record for most inversions with Colossus at Thorpe Park in Surrey, England.
